intro: Tweedia andina, also known as Tweedia, is a perennial plant species native to the Andes mountains in South America, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. Tweedia andina contains bioactive constituents like cardiac glycosides, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
